items.label: Invalid / unknown
items.description: Reserved sentinel: tooling replaces LLM outputs that are not valid course_type ids so ingest can continue.

items.label: Design studio
items.description: A project-based studio course that normally carries larger curricular weight than other courses.
rules: Use only when the course is clearly a project-based studio.
Do not classify a course as design_studio merely because it includes a project.

Use design_studio only when normalized_unit_weight is strictly greater than 1.1; at 1.1 or below (or when weight is unknown), use design_methods_systems instead. Higher weight above 1.1 should increase the likelihood of choosing design_studio when the course is clearly a heavy studio/project.
Design studio can never be hybrid.
If primary_type is design_studio, secondary_type must be null.

items.label: Human factors
items.description: Courses focused on people, communities, users, behavior, culture, social context, design research, ethnography, ergonomics, or human-centered design.

items.label: Technology
items.description: Courses focused on technical design methods, computational tools, software, hardware, fabrication, sensors, AI, robotics, CAD, optimization, or technologies used in the design itself.

items.label: History, theory, and positioning
items.description: Courses focused on design history, design theory, critical theory, contemporary issues, ethics, politics, movements, disciplinary framing, or colloquia.

items.label: Rhetoric, storytelling, and presentation
items.description: Courses focused on writing, presenting, communicating, documenting, exhibiting, pitching, or narrating design work.

items.label: Design methods and systems
items.description: Courses focused on design process, structured methods, systems thinking, strategy, problem framing, research synthesis, service design methods, participatory design, systems mapping, or design frameworks.

items.label: Entrepreneurship
items.description: Courses focused on venture creation, innovation management, commercialization, markets, business models, product-market fit, leadership, startup practice, or go-to-market strategy.

items.label: Open or other
items.description: Use when a course or elective slot is broad, unspecified, intentionally flexible, or does not clearly map to one of the defined types.
